title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text: Paper is such a common material that we rarely (0) a second thought to its origins. However, its invention was one of the most significant (1) in human history. Paper was first developed in ancient China around 100 BC, though it took centuries for the technique to (2) to other parts of the world. Before paper, people wrote on materials such as bamboo, silk, or animal skins, which were either expensive or (3) to produce. The introduction of paper revolutionized the way information was recorded and (4)... It made books more accessible and helped spread knowledge and ideas across cultures. The (5) for paper grew rapidly during the printing revolution in Europe. Today, despite the rise of digital technology, paper remains essential in many (6) of life. However, concerns about deforestation have led to increased efforts to recycle paper and find (7) materials. The paper industry has had to adapt to these environmental challenges while continuing to (8) the world's demand for this versatile product.

example.explanation: 'Give a thought' is an expression that means to think or consider something.

questions.explanation: 'Developments' is the most appropriate noun to refer to important historical advances or achievements.
'Spread to' is the correct verb to describe how something spreads to other areas.
'Difficult to produce' is the most natural collocation in this context.
'Shared' is the appropriate verb to describe how information is shared.
'Demand for' is the correct collocation to refer to the demand for a product.
'Areas of life' is the standard expression to refer to different areas of life.
'Alternative materials' is the correct collocation to refer to materials that can replace others.
'Meet demand' is a fixed expression that means meet the demand.
